description: Compile-only firmware validation targets (FW-COMPILE-MATRIX-001). Source-of-truth list of product YAMLs that CI may compile to validate YAML / config / codegen buildability before hardware is available. This file is a CI validation lane only: compile success is necessary but not sufficient for preview / stable readiness. It does not expose new WebFlash builds, create release artifacts, import firmware, promote any product, or add any product YAML. See docs/compile-only-firmware-validation.md.

targets.reason: Currently committed Release-One stable WebFlash build. Compile-only validation pins that the canonical Release-One YAML still composes / substitutes / includes / generates code cleanly under the current ESPHome version, independent of hardware availability.
targets.notes: Verified-for-release-one product (config/product-catalog.json status=production, webflash_build_matrix=true). This compile-only target does NOT add a new WebFlash exposure, flip webflash_build_matrix, or change the committed build entry; it only validates buildability.

targets.reason: Currently committed LED-bearing preview WebFlash build. Compile-only validation pins that the LED-bearing canonical YAML still composes / substitutes / includes / generates code cleanly under the current ESPHome version, independent of hardware availability. Compile success is not LED-stable proof; the LED stable gauntlet (S360-300-BENCH-001, WF-HW-TEST-001, WF-HW-TEST-003, RELEASE-007) remains open.
targets.notes: Preview-only product (config/product-catalog.json status=preview, channel=preview, webflash_build_matrix=true). This compile-only target does NOT promote the LED preview to stable, change the committed build entry, or claim hardware proof exists.

targets.reason: POE non-fan compile-confidence target (FW-COMPILE-POE-NONFAN-001). Validates the minimum Core ceiling + PoE PSU + base + health package composition that the WebFlash-shipping Release-One build already proves compiles. Compile success is not WebFlash exposure, not a release artifact, not S360-410 schematic verification, not Release-One PoE caveat closure, and not product-catalog / stable / preview promotion.
targets.notes: Skeleton lives under products/compile-only/ so the top-level product-catalog enumeration gate at tests/test_product_catalog.py::_top_level_product_yamls does not require a config/product-catalog.json entry. No webflash_wrapper, no artifact_name, no entry in config/webflash-builds.json.

targets.reason: POE non-fan compile-confidence target (FW-COMPILE-POE-NONFAN-001). Validates the Core ceiling + PoE PSU + RoomIQ (comfort_ceiling + presence_ceiling) package composition stripped of VentIQ. Same RoomIQ subset that the WebFlash-shipping Release-One build already exercises. Compile success is not WebFlash exposure, not a release artifact, and not product-catalog / stable / preview promotion.
targets.notes: Skeleton lives under products/compile-only/ so the top-level product-catalog enumeration gate does not require a config/product-catalog.json entry. No webflash_wrapper, no artifact_name, no entry in config/webflash-builds.json.

targets.reason: POE non-fan compile-confidence target (FW-COMPILE-POE-NONFAN-001). Validates the Core ceiling + PoE PSU + VentIQ (airiq_bathroom_base + bathroom_profile) package composition stripped of RoomIQ. Same VentIQ subset that the WebFlash-shipping Release-One build already exercises. Compile success is not WebFlash exposure, not a release artifact, and not product-catalog / stable / preview promotion.
targets.notes: Skeleton lives under products/compile-only/ so the top-level product-catalog enumeration gate does not require a config/product-catalog.json entry. No webflash_wrapper, no artifact_name, no entry in config/webflash-builds.json.

targets.reason: POE non-fan compile-confidence target (FW-COMPILE-POE-NONFAN-001). Validates the Core ceiling + PoE PSU + AirIQ (airiq_ceiling + airiq_basic_profile) package composition. The AirIQ + comfort + presence package composition is already proven expressible by products/sense360-core-ceiling.yaml. Compile success is not WebFlash exposure, not a release artifact, and not AirIQ stable / preview promotion.
targets.notes: Skeleton lives under products/compile-only/ so the top-level product-catalog enumeration gate does not require a config/product-catalog.json entry. No webflash_wrapper, no artifact_name, no entry in config/webflash-builds.json. AirIQ and VentIQ are mutually exclusive; this target uses AirIQ.

targets.reason: POE non-fan compile-confidence target (FW-COMPILE-POE-NONFAN-001). Validates the Core ceiling + PoE PSU + AirIQ + RoomIQ package composition. The AirIQ + comfort + presence composition is already proven expressible by products/sense360-core-ceiling.yaml. Compile success is not WebFlash exposure, not a release artifact, and not AirIQ stable / preview promotion.
targets.notes: Skeleton lives under products/compile-only/ so the top-level product-catalog enumeration gate does not require a config/product-catalog.json entry. No webflash_wrapper, no artifact_name, no entry in config/webflash-builds.json. AirIQ and VentIQ are mutually exclusive; this target uses AirIQ.

targets.reason: FanRelay (S360-310) compile-only validation target (FW-COMPILE-RELAY-001). Validates that the PRODUCT-RELAY-001 / PR #564 product YAML (advanced / manual-warning-only sibling of Release-One; composes Core ceiling + PoE PSU + VentIQ + RoomIQ + FanRelay; inherits ${relay_pin} → GPIO3 from the parent Core abstract package post-CORE-ABSTRACT-BUS-001A) still composes / substitutes / includes / generates code cleanly under the current ESPHome version. Compile success is NOT WebFlash exposure, NOT a release artifact, NOT WebFlash import readiness, NOT a RELEASE-RELAY-001 unblock, NOT compliance approval, NOT hardware proof, NOT a competent-person sign-off, NOT a default kit / not recommended, and NOT stable / preview promotion. The advanced / manual-warning posture defined by PRODUCT-RELAY-001-READINESS-REFRESH / PR #563 and confirmed by WEBFLASH-RELAY-001-READINESS-REFRESH / PR #565 stays in force: WebFlash exposure remains blocked behind the seven remaining WebFlash gates owned by a separate WEBFLASH-RELAY-001 slice. This target sits in the compile-only validation lane only.
targets.notes: FanRelay product YAML lives at products/sense360-ceiling-poe-ventiq-fanrelay-roomiq.yaml (NOT under products/webflash/, NOT under products/compile-only/). The matching catalog row in config/product-catalog.json (config_string: Ceiling-POE-VentIQ-FanRelay-RoomIQ, status: hardware-pending, webflash_build_matrix: false, no artifact_name, no webflash_wrapper) was landed by PRODUCT-RELAY-001 / PR #564. The FanRelay token is NOT in config/webflash-builds.json (no FanRelay build entry exists). The FanRelay config string is NOT in release_one_required_configs. No WebFlash wrapper exists under products/webflash/. The S360-KIT-BATH-RELAY kit stays future-expansion / hardware-pending / webflash_exposure_allowed_now: false / stable_ready_now: false. WEBFLASH-RELAY-001, RELEASE-RELAY-001, and WF-IMPORT-RELAY-001 stay blocked behind their own gates. MANUAL-FIRMWARE-CANDIDATE-001 (2026-05-27) records compile_validation_status: validated-full-compile for this top-level FanRelay product compile-only target from the already-committed FW-FULL-COMPILE-TOPLEVEL-FANS-001 / PR #616 full-lane run (this product YAML was target #8, rc=0, Flash 53.8% / 987219 bytes); it is recorded from that committed evidence, not a new compile run, and none is fabricated. This makes the FanRelay top-level product YAML a manual / no-WebFlash firmware candidate alongside FanPWM / FanDAC. It stays compile-only: NOT WebFlash exposure, NOT a release artifact, NOT hardware-stable, NOT compliance / competent-person approval, NOT kit-default / recommended.

targets.reason: FanDAC (S360-312) compile-only validation target (FW-COMPILE-DAC-001). Exercises the FanDAC package composition (two GP8403 dual-channel I2C DACs, four neutral outputs; canonical alias packages/expansions/fan_dac.yaml -> fan_gp8403.yaml) on the proven Core ceiling + PoE PSU + base + health base after PACKAGE-DAC-001 / PR #573 implemented the package and CORE-ABSTRACT-BUS-001B landed the core_i2c bus the GP8403 peripheral binds. This target resolves the gp8403 voltage enum concern raised by PR #574: the package fan_dac_1_output_range / fan_dac_2_output_range substitutions are fixed from the invalid ESPHome enum string '0-10V' to the valid '10V' (customer-facing 0-10V) per https://esphome.io/components/output/gp8403, matching the default '10V' the output-range policy already implied (docs/hardware/s360-312-r4-fandac.md row 6). The full ESPHome compile is validated by run 26364679370 (workflow_dispatch / compile_mode=full, 9 compile-only targets, conclusion success): scripts/validate_compile_targets.py --compile ran against this YAML in CI and passed. Compile success is NOT WebFlash exposure, NOT a release artifact, NOT WebFlash import readiness, NOT a PRODUCT-DAC-001 / WEBFLASH-DAC-001 / RELEASE-DAC-001 unblock, NOT S360-312 schematic / BOM verification, NOT hardware proof, and NOT stable / preview promotion.
targets.notes: FanDAC compile-only skeleton lives at products/compile-only/ceiling-poe-fandac.yaml (NOT under products/webflash/, NOT a top-level products/ product YAML). No config/product-catalog.json entry is added (the products/compile-only/ subdirectory is excluded from the top-level catalog enumeration gate), so NO normal DAC product YAML is added; PRODUCT-DAC-001 stays gated on this validation. The FanDAC token is NOT in config/webflash-builds.json (no FanDAC build entry exists). The Ceiling-POE-FanDAC config string is NOT in release_one_required_configs. No WebFlash wrapper exists under products/webflash/. No webflash_build_matrix flip, no artifact_name. compile_validation_status: validated-full-compile records that the full ESPHome compile passed in run 26364679370 (workflow_dispatch / compile_mode=full, 9 targets, conclusion success), superseding the prior pending-ci marker; voltage_enum_fixed: true records that the gp8403 voltage enum concern is resolved at the package layer (10V, customer-facing 0-10V), not merely tracked. A single GP8403 cannot drive one output at 0-5V and the other at 0-10V (one V5V reference / one range register per chip); this target does not claim that.

targets.reason: FanPWM (S360-311) compile-only validation target (FW-COMPILE-PWM-001). Exercises the PWM-drive-only FanPWM package composition (four independent SX1509 PWM-drive fan-speed controllers; canonical packages/expansions/fan_pwm.yaml composing the neutral binding packages/expansions/fan_pwm_sx1509.yaml) on the proven Core ceiling + PoE PSU + base + health base after PACKAGE-PWM-001-IMPLEMENT-001 / PR #590 reconciled the package to the PWM-drive-only scope and CORE-ABSTRACT-BUS-001B landed the core_i2c bus the SX1509 expander binds. Validates that the package composes / substitutes / includes / generates code cleanly under the current ESPHome version: the SX1509 hub on core_i2c, the four output: platform: sx1509 PWM-drive outputs (fan_pwm_drive_1..4 on channels 0..3), and the four fan: platform: speed controllers (fan_pwm_1..4). The package wires NO pulse_counter and NO RPM sensor (an SX1509 expander pin is compile-proven unable to back an ESPHome pulse_counter per PWM-SX1509-TACH-PROOF-001 / PR #589; esphome config rejects it with '[sx1509] is an invalid option for [pin]'); the four Pul_Cou1..4 lines stay as the binding's INTERNAL diagnostic binary GPIO states (fan_pwm_tach_1..4), never surfaced as RPM. TachIO / GPIO16 stays reserved/pending. The full ESPHome compile is validated by run 26414398902 (Compile-only Firmware Validation; compile_mode=full, 10 compile-only targets, conclusion success): the 'Run compile-only validator (full compile)' step ran scripts/validate_compile_targets.py --compile against this YAML in CI and passed. Compile success is NOT WebFlash exposure, NOT a release artifact, NOT WebFlash import readiness, NOT a PRODUCT-PWM-001 / WEBFLASH-PWM-001 / RELEASE-PWM-001 unblock, NOT S360-311 schematic / BOM / bench (PWM polarity, current/thermal envelope) verification, NOT RPM support, NOT hardware proof, and NOT stable / preview promotion.
targets.notes: FanPWM compile-only skeleton lives at products/compile-only/ceiling-poe-fanpwm.yaml (NOT under products/webflash/, NOT a top-level products/ product YAML). No config/product-catalog.json entry is added (the products/compile-only/ subdirectory is excluded from the top-level catalog enumeration gate), so NO normal FanPWM product YAML is added; PRODUCT-PWM-001 stays gated. The FanPWM token is NOT in config/webflash-builds.json (no FanPWM build entry exists). The Ceiling-POE-FanPWM config string is NOT in release_one_required_configs. No WebFlash wrapper exists under products/webflash/. No webflash_build_matrix flip, no artifact_name, no release artifact. compile_validation_status: validated-full-compile records that the full ESPHome compile passed in run 26414398902 (Compile-only Firmware Validation; compile_mode=full, 10 targets, conclusion success; the 'Run compile-only validator (full compile)' step ran scripts/validate_compile_targets.py --compile against this YAML and passed), superseding the prior pending-ci marker. This records the compile-only result ONLY; it is NOT WebFlash exposure, NOT WebFlash import, NOT a release artifact, NOT compliance approval, and NOT hardware-stable readiness — webflash_exposure_allowed_now stays false and shipment_status stays compile-only. rpm_supported: false records that the PWM-drive-only package exposes no per-fan or aggregate RPM and no pulse_counter; this target makes no RPM claim. The S360-311 / FanPWM lane stays deferred (config/compile-only-candidates.json Ceiling-POE-FanPWM keeps candidate_status defer) pending PRODUCT-PWM-001 and the S360-311 bench gates; a single validation target is not lane-wide compile-only readiness.

targets.reason: FanDAC (S360-312) TOP-LEVEL product-YAML compile-only validation target (TOPLEVEL-FAN-COMPILE-TARGETS-001). Registers the canonical FanDAC product YAML landed by PRODUCT-DAC-001 (products/sense360-ceiling-poe-fandac.yaml; the consumer-facing deliverable, NOT the products/compile-only/ceiling-poe-fandac.yaml skeleton) directly into the compile-only validation lane so the actual top-level product YAML carries its own compile-only coverage, closing the FanDAC top-level full-compile evidence gap that the skeleton-only registration left open. This mirrors the FanRelay precedent (ceiling-poe-ventiq-fanrelay-roomiq-compile-only registers the top-level FanRelay product YAML). The product YAML composes the IDENTICAL Core ceiling + PoE PSU + base/health + canonical FanDAC alias packages/expansions/fan_dac.yaml -> fan_gp8403.yaml package set as the already-full-compile-validated skeleton (proven by tests/test_dac_product_readiness.py composition-parity tests). The full ESPHome compile of THIS registered top-level target is now recorded green by FW-FULL-COMPILE-TOPLEVEL-FANS-001 (LOCAL run 2026-05-27, ESPHome 2026.4.5, board esp32-s3-devkitc-1 / framework espidf; scripts/validate_compile_targets.py --compile, 12 targets, all rc=0; this target rc=0, Flash 50.6% / 927815 bytes, real firmware.bin), so compile_validation_status: validated-full-compile now stands, superseding the prior pending-ci marker. This was a LOCAL run; no GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch run id exists for it and none is fabricated. Compile coverage is NOT WebFlash exposure, NOT a release artifact, NOT WebFlash import readiness, NOT a WEBFLASH-DAC-001 / RELEASE-DAC-001 / WF-IMPORT-DAC-001 unblock, NOT S360-312 schematic / BOM verification, NOT hardware proof, NOT compliance approval, NOT Cloudlift-ready, and NOT stable / preview promotion.
targets.notes: Top-level FanDAC product-YAML compile-only target. product_yaml is products/sense360-ceiling-poe-fandac.yaml (a top-level products/ product YAML with a config/product-catalog.json row from PRODUCT-DAC-001, status: hardware-pending, webflash_build_matrix: false, no artifact_name, no webflash_wrapper). This is a SEPARATE registration from the ceiling-poe-fandac-compile-only skeleton (products/compile-only/ceiling-poe-fandac.yaml), which is preserved unchanged and stays compile_validation_status: validated-full-compile; both target the Ceiling-POE-FanDAC config string but are distinct files with distinct ids. The skeleton is the historical CI validation file; this row makes the actual shipped product YAML a first-class compile-only target. compile_validation_status: validated-full-compile records that THIS registered top-level target was compiled by a full ESPHome --compile run and passed: FW-FULL-COMPILE-TOPLEVEL-FANS-001 ran scripts/validate_compile_targets.py --compile (the compile-only.yml workflow_dispatch / compile_mode=full command) LOCALLY on 2026-05-27 with ESPHome 2026.4.5 (board esp32-s3-devkitc-1, framework espidf / ESP-IDF 5.5.4) against commit 17caa86 on claude/toplevel-fans-compile-validation-n8LRb; all 12 registered targets passed (rc=0, 0 skipped, 0 failures) and this target produced a real firmware.bin (Flash 50.6% / 927815 bytes), superseding the prior pending-ci marker. This was a LOCAL run; no GitHub Actions run id exists and none is fabricated. (The skeleton ceiling-poe-fandac-compile-only stays separately validated-full-compile via run 26364679370.) No WebFlash exposure, no webflash_build_matrix flip, no artifact_name, no release artifact, no FanDAC entry in config/webflash-builds.json, not in release_one_required_configs. Enforces the FanDAC <-> AirIQ mutex inherited from the product YAML; no simultaneous per-output 0-5V + 0-10V on a single GP8403 is claimed.

targets.reason: FanPWM (S360-311) NATIVE ESP32-S3 GPIO compile-only validation target (S360-311-NATIVE-FANPWM-YAML-001). Exercises the NATIVE FanPWM package composition (packages/expansions/fan_pwm_native.yaml) on the proven Core ceiling + PoE PSU + base + health base: four native output: platform: ledc PWM-drive outputs (TachPMW1..4 -> native ESP32-S3 IO10 / IO11 / IO12 / IO39) feeding four fan: platform: speed controllers, plus three native sensor: platform: pulse_counter tach inputs (Pul_Cou1 / Pul_Cou2 / Pul_Cou4 -> native ESP32-S3 IO17 / IO18 / IO9) as INTERNAL diagnostic pulse-rate inputs only. This is the native-GPIO successor to the legacy / superseded SX1509 composition: it does NOT use the SX1509 for PWM output and does NOT use the SX1509 for any tach / pulse_counter input, per the canonical map in docs/hardware/s360-100-native-fan-gpio-map.md (S360-100-NATIVE-FAN-GPIO-MAP-001) established by PR #634 / #637 / #638 / #639. Pul_Cou3 (native IO46) is DISABLED / TBD because IO46 collides with the Core fan_status_led_pin (GPIO46); TachIO (native IO16) is RESERVED / PENDING (ambiguous shared-passthrough role). No GPIO numbers are invented; every pin is the schematic-printed value recorded by S360-100-NATIVE-FAN-GPIO-MAP-001 (none bench-verified). Compile coverage is NOT WebFlash exposure, NOT a release artifact, NOT WebFlash import readiness, NOT a WEBFLASH-PWM-001 / RELEASE-PWM-001 / WF-IMPORT-PWM-001 unblock, NOT S360-311 schematic / BOM / bench (PWM polarity, current/thermal envelope) verification, NOT RPM / tach support, NOT hardware proof, NOT compliance approval, and NOT stable / preview promotion.
targets.notes: Native ESP32-S3 GPIO FanPWM compile-only skeleton at products/compile-only/ceiling-poe-fanpwm-native.yaml (NOT under products/webflash/, NOT a top-level products/ product YAML). No config/product-catalog.json entry is added (the products/compile-only/ subdirectory is excluded from the top-level catalog enumeration gate), so NO normal native FanPWM product YAML is added. This is a SEPARATE registration from the legacy SX1509 skeleton (products/compile-only/ceiling-poe-fanpwm.yaml, id ceiling-poe-fanpwm-compile-only) and the legacy top-level product target (id ceiling-poe-fanpwm-product-compile-only); both legacy targets are preserved unchanged and stay validated-full-compile against the legacy SX1509 composition (run 26414398902). compile_validation_status: validated-full-compile records that a full ESPHome --compile run was performed against THIS native composition and PASSED, superseding the prior pending-ci marker: S360-311-NATIVE-FANPWM-COMPILE-001 ran `esphome compile products/compile-only/ceiling-poe-fanpwm-native.yaml` (the per-target invocation equivalent to scripts/validate_compile_targets.py --compile for this single target; the native target only, not the full 13-target matrix) LOCALLY on 2026-05-28 with ESPHome 2026.4.5 (board esp32-s3-devkitc-1, framework espidf / ESP-IDF v5.5.4 / framework-espidf 3.50504.0, platform-espressif32 55.03.38-1) against commit 643bbd3 (643bbd3b2c1f798715800623b1bc4ba783b21599) on claude/native-fanpwm-compile-result-hPUwn; the run returned rc=0 ('INFO Successfully compiled program.') and produced a real firmware.bin (RAM 13.2% / 43156 bytes of 327680; Flash 51.7% / 948679 bytes of 1835008). This was a LOCAL run; no GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch run id exists for it and none is fabricated (the egress-restricted environment cannot trigger the compile-only.yml workflow). The legacy SX1509 full-compile run 26414398902 does NOT transfer to this native composition; this row is the native composition's own compile proof. compile_validation_status: validated-full-compile is compile coverage ONLY: NOT WebFlash exposure, NOT WebFlash import, NOT a release artifact, NOT compliance / competent-person approval, and NOT hardware-stable readiness — webflash_exposure_allowed_now stays false and shipment_status stays compile-only. rpm_supported: false records that the native pulse_counter inputs are internal diagnostic pulse-rate inputs only — no per-fan or aggregate RPM is surfaced and no RPM claim is made; RPM / tach support stays UNVALIDATED until measured bench evidence exists (a green compile is NOT RPM / tach bench validation). The FanPWM token is NOT in config/webflash-builds.json. The Ceiling-POE-FanPWM config string is NOT in release_one_required_configs. No WebFlash wrapper, no webflash_build_matrix flip, no artifact_name, no release artifact, no firmware/sources.json or manifest.json change. S360-311 schematic_status stays cataloged_unverified and the bench gates (PWM polarity; per-fan / aggregate current + thermal envelope) remain open.
